item.title: Honda (American Honda Motor Co.): SEAT BELTS:FRONT Recall (2024 honda accord)
item.summary: Honda (American Honda Motor Co.) is recalling certain 2023-2024 Accord and HR-V vehicles. The front seat belt pretensioners may be missing the rivet that secures the quick connector and wire plate. As such, these vehicles fail to comply with the requirements of Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standard numbers 208, "Occupant Crash Protection," 209, "Seat Belt Assembles," and 210, "Seat Belt Assembly Anchorages."
item.affectedPeople: A seat belt pretensioner missing a rivet will not properly restrain the occupant, increasing the risk of injury during a crash.
item.remedy: Dealers will inspect and replace the seat belt pretensioner assemblies as necessary, free of charge. Owner notification letters were mailed January 8, 2024. Owners may contact Honda customer service at 1-888-234-2138. Honda's numbers for this recall are MG7 and NG5.
item.payoutAmount: Free repair, remedy, software update, or dealer service if stated by the NHTSA recall
